title: United States attacks Iran for sixth consecutive day and tension rises in the Strait of Hormuz
summary: The military offensive seeks to weaken Iran, which maintains an inflexible stance, while alternative routes for crude oil are being sought.

title: Nikkei index plunges more than 2,500 points over fears of AI bubble
summary: The fall of technology stocks and doubts about the growth of artificial intelligence triggered a massive sell-off on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

title: Japan passes law punishing desecration of the national flag
summary: The law establishes prison sentences and fines for those who damage or soil the flag, although criticisms about its constitutionality persist.

title: Gion festival float parade begins in Kyoto with 23 magnificent floats
summary: The traditional Gion festival parade marks the start of summer festivities in the city of Kyoto.

title: Nichirei resumes logistics operations after cyberattack
summary: The cold logistics company partially restored its services after suffering a computer attack that affected its systems.

title: Imperial succession reform proposal divides parties in Japan
summary: The proposal to allow women to inherit the throne generates controversy and faces obstacles in Parliament.

title: Ohtani, Yamamoto and Sasaki define their roles for the second half of the MLB season
summary: The Japanese players prepare for the resumption of the regular season, with Ohtani as designated hitter.

title: Argentina defeats England and will face Spain in the tournament final
summary: The Argentine team, led by Messi, beat England in the semifinals and will seek the title against Spain.

title: Deer population in Nara park reaches historic record
summary: Overfeeding by tourists has caused an unprecedented increase in the number of deer.

title: Ruling party extends parliamentary session until July 25
summary: The extension seeks to pass pending laws, but the opposition criticizes the lack of progress on key issues.

title: Andy Burnham elected leader of the Labour Party and will become prime minister on July 20
summary: The new Labour leader will replace Keir Starmer after winning the party's internal elections.

title: Debate on constitutional reform in Japan broadcast on television for the first time
summary: The parties present their positions on amending the Constitution, while the prime minister insists on the need for changes.

title: Trump teleprompter technician investigated for illegal betting
summary: The suspended employee allegedly used privileged information from speeches to bet on prediction markets.

title: Reform of criminal case review system advances in Japanese Parliament
summary: The bill that restricts requests for judicial review generates concern among lawyers and jurists.

title: Yen falls to 162 yen per dollar, its lowest level in months
summary: The Japanese currency weakens due to the strengthening dollar and expectations of rate hikes in the United States.

title: Japanese scientist Susumu Tonegawa, Nobel Prize in Medicine, dies
summary: The immunologist awarded the Nobel Prize in 1987 died leaving a legacy in immunology.

title: Kim Jong-un meets with senior Chinese Communist Party official to strengthen cooperation
summary: The North Korean leader and China's fourth-ranking official agreed to boost bilateral ties.

title: France approves bill authorizing assisted suicide
summary: The legislation adds to the European trend of legalizing active euthanasia, generating ethical debate.

title: Saudi Arabia to launch direct flights to Tokyo starting in November
summary: The new air route aims to boost tourism and business between Saudi Arabia and Japan.

title: United States imposes four-year stay limit for international students
summary: The new rule restricts the duration of student visas, affecting thousands of foreigners.
